type Locale = 'en' | 'pt';
type Translations = typeof oEn;
const oTranslations: Record<Locale, Translations> = { en: oEn, pt: oPt };

interface I18nContextType {
  locale: Locale;
  setLocale: (locale: Locale) => void;
  t: (key: string, replacements?: Record<string, string | number>) => string;
}

const I18nContext = createContext<I18nContextType | undefined>(undefined);

const fnGetInitialLocale = (): Locale => {
  if (typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.navigator) {
    const sBrowserLang = window.navigator.language.toLowerCase();
    if (sBrowserLang.startsWith('pt')) return 'pt';
  }
  return 'en';
};

const fnGetNestedTranslation = (oObj: any, sKey: string): string | undefined => {
  return sKey.split('.').reduce((o, i) => (o ? o[i] : undefined), oObj);
};

export const I18nProvider: React.FC<{ children: React.ReactNode }> = ({ children: oChildren }) => {
  const [sLocale, fnSetLocale] = useState<Locale>(fnGetInitialLocale());

  const fnT = useCallback((sKey: string, oReplacements?: Record<string, string | number>): string => {
    let sTranslation = fnGetNestedTranslation(oTranslations[sLocale], sKey) || fnGetNestedTranslation(oTranslations['en'], sKey) || sKey;
    if (oReplacements) {
        Object.keys(oReplacements).forEach(sRKey => {
            sTranslation = sTranslation.replace(`{{${sRKey}}}`, String(oReplacements[sRKey]));
        });
    }
    return sTranslation;
  }, [sLocale]);

  const oValue = useMemo(() => ({
    locale: sLocale,
    setLocale: fnSetLocale,
    t: fnT
  }), [sLocale, fnT]);

  return (
    <I18nContext.Provider value={oValue}>
      {oChildren}
    </I18nContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useI18n = (): I18nContextType => {
  const context = useContext(I18nContext);
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error('useI18n must be used within an I18nProvider');
  }
  return context;
};

payload.text: In the 'Finishing Touches' section, when a Predator Type is selected, display its associated advantages and flaws more prominently, perhaps in a dedicated card below the selection. Also, clearly indicate any automatic discipline dots or specialty choices granted by the predator type.

**Highlighting Advantage/Flaw**

I'm focusing on the "Predator Type" selection UI within character creation. My current goal is to make the related advantages and flaws much more noticeable. The user feedback is guiding my efforts.


**Enhancing Presentation of Effects**

I'm now implementing the dedicated card to showcase the advantages, flaws, automatic discipline dots, and specialty choices granted by a selected "Predator Type". My plan is to hook into the `fnHandlePredatorTypeChange` function within `App.tsx`.


**Refining Card Components**

I'm now integrating the data into the dedicated card to display the "Predator Type" effects. I'm focusing on populating the card with advantages, flaws, and the specific choices of disciplines. I'm using the `oSelectedPredator` object to dynamically render this information. I am also planning on formatting the information into 2-columns on wider screens.


**Displaying Humanity & Discipline**

I've just added the "Humanity Modifier" to the display, using color-coding to highlight positive, negative, or neutral values. I've also integrated the bonus discipline display, showing the number of dots and the discipline itself using a similar color-coded structure. I am still focusing on ensuring this information is clear and legible.


**Implementing Advantage/Flaw Styling**

I am now rendering the advantages and flaws for the selected "Predator Type". I'm using distinct visual styles. Advantages are green-themed, and flaws are red-themed, and include a cost display. This uses Tailwind classes for a modern look.


**Refining Flaw Display**

I'm currently working to improve the presentation of flaws. I've implemented a new style to emphasize mandatory flaws, using red-themed styling for visual clarity, which I'm now testing. Additionally, I am currently styling the cost display for the flaws to make them more apparent.


**Assessing Current UI Presentation**

I'm now re-evaluating the degree of prominence, given that there's already a dedicated section within a `GothicFrame`. I need to determine if a separate "card" is truly necessary, or if enhancements within the existing area would suffice, considering the user's specific request for increased visibility. My next step will be to test the current design and compare it with the desired user experience.


**Revising UI Presentation**

I'm now reconsidering the need for a dedicated card, as the existing UI might already have the desired prominence. The provided code reflects my previous changes, so the question is how much more emphasis is required? I'm re-evaluating the user's need for a "dedicated card" versus enhancements to the existing presentation. This involves testing and comparing designs to ensure a clear user experience. I'll focus on improving the layout and possibly updating translations for better labeling. The existing logic looks sound.


**Updating UI Features**

I am now aiming to make the presentation of Predator Type benefits more impactful. I'm focusing on icons and colors to distinguish "Granted" versus "Choice" elements, especially with the Humanity, Disciplines, and Specialties summaries. I will review `lib/i18n.tsx` for relevant translations and add more specific labels. I'm also planning to update `App.tsx` and integrate the Predator Type benefits into the character sheet. I will make a separate sub-selector for Specialty and Discipline choices.


**Adding Specialty Choice**

I'm now integrating a mechanism for the user to select specialties related to their chosen predator type. I'm focusing on enhancing the "Predator Details" box and adding a choice element within it. I'm leveraging the existing `specialties` array in the `Character` type. I'll need to update `App.tsx` and integrate the ability to choose an option from `specialtyOptions`.


**Summarizing Discipline Gains**

I'm now incorporating a summary of the gained disciplines in the "Finishing Touches" step within `App.tsx`, showing the effect from the `oSelectedPredator` selection if options exist.


**Integrating Specialty Options**

I'm now focusing on the integration of specialty choices, which relates to a specific user selection. I'm updating `App.tsx` and the `Character` type to facilitate this, aiming to allow for a flexible selection of specialties for each predator type. The existing `specialties` array in the `Character` type will be leveraged.


**Refining Advantage Display**

I'm now refining the display of advantages, ensuring they stand out more. I'm focusing on the green-themed styling I have implemented, and how I can utilize it to make the advantages more apparent. I'm also using an icon to provide a better visual cue. I'm planning to make sure the costs are noticeable.


**Improving Flaw Presentation**

I'm now focusing on improving the flaws section. I've been aiming for greater clarity in the presentation, with an emphasis on mandatory flaws. I've implemented a new style to emphasize these flaws and have refined the styling of the cost display. I am now working on using the styling and other features to further emphasize the flaws section to make it more apparent to the user.


**Evaluating Flaw Presentation**

I'm now iterating on the flaws, aiming for clearer display. I've been improving the presentation of the mandatory flaws to draw the user's focus. I'm focusing on ensuring all the flaws are easily visible. I've refined the styling and the cost display to emphasize the flaws and highlight mandatory ones.


**Implementing Specialty Selection**

I'm now implementing the specialty selection logic within the UI. I'm focusing on the toggle behavior, making sure the user's selected specialties are correctly updated. I'm using filtering to manage the selections, and I'm updating the character data to ensure selections are persisted correctly. The code utilizes conditional styling for visual feedback.

Added clear visual grouping for humanity modifiers, discipline bonuses, granted advantages/flaws, and interactive buttons for picking mandatory predator specialties.

payload.text: Os poderes não estão bem descritos. Tem so o caminho ali, nada de descrição

**Clarifying Localization Paths**

I'm currently focused on the issue of discipline powers in the character sheet. Instead of displaying the human-readable names, the system incorrectly shows localization paths like `disciplines.animalism.powers.senseTheBeast.name`. I am thinking about how to actually render the name and description properly.


**Analyzing Power Data Fields**

I'm now diving into the `constants.ts` file, specifically the `fnGetP` function. I'm scrutinizing how the `id`, `name`, and `description` fields are constructed for discipline powers. My hunch is the issue lies within how the translation strings are being accessed or integrated within this function. I'm focusing on the `fnT` function and how it interfaces with the internationalization logic in `lib/i18n.tsx`. The goal is to ensure the correct human-readable names are being retrieved and associated with each power.


**Pinpointing Missing Translations**

I've zeroed in on the likely root cause. My analysis of `fnGetP` confirms that the issue stems from missing, specific nested keys within the Portuguese translation data. The screenshot reveals the application is displaying localization paths, implying the i18n system is returning keys when translations are absent. Comparing Portuguese (`oPt`) to English (`oEn`) reveals a discrepancy: the Portuguese translation object is missing the `.powers` nested object under each discipline, which is present in English. This explains the erroneous output.


**Revising Translation Data**

I've shifted focus to updating `lib/i18n.tsx`. It turns out that both the English and Portuguese objects were overly simplified, and were missing the `.powers` definitions. I must incorporate the correct nested power definitions for all 12 disciplines, using keys from `constants.ts`. I will select a few iconic powers for each level and discipline, using `Animalism` as a starting point.


**Expanding Power Definitions**

Now, I'm diligently expanding the power definitions within `lib/i18n.tsx`. I have included all powers for Auspex, Celerity, Dominate, Fortitude, Obfuscate, Potence, and Presence. I've also begun to implement the necessary structure for the remaining disciplines, ensuring that I capture the correct keys and power names for each level and discipline. I'm focusing on ensuring consistency and completeness.

O erro visual que você está vendo ocorre porque as chaves de tradução dos poderes das disciplinas (como `senseTheBeast`, `feralWhispers`, etc.) não foram incluídas nos objetos de tradução `oEn` e `oPt` no arquivo `lib/i18n.tsx`. Quando o sistema não encontra a tradução, ele retorna o próprio caminho da chave.

Vou atualizar o arquivo `lib/i18n.tsx` com as descrições detalhadas para todos os poderes das disciplinas em Inglês e Português, garantindo que a ficha de personagem exiba os nomes e efeitos corretamente.

type Locale = 'en' | 'pt';
type Translations = typeof oEn;
const oTranslations: Record<Locale, Translations> = { en: oEn, pt: oPt };

interface I18nContextType {
  locale: Locale;
  setLocale: (locale: Locale) => void;
  t: (key: string, replacements?: Record<string, string | number>) => string;
}

const I18nContext = createContext<I18nContextType | undefined>(undefined);

const fnGetInitialLocale = (): Locale => {
  if (typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.navigator) {
    const sBrowserLang = window.navigator.language.toLowerCase();
    if (sBrowserLang.startsWith('pt')) return 'pt';
  }
  return 'en';
};

const fnGetNestedTranslation = (oObj: any, sKey: string): string | undefined => {
  return sKey.split('.').reduce((o, i) => (o ? o[i] : undefined), oObj);
};

export const I18nProvider: React.FC<{ children: React.ReactNode }> = ({ children: oChildren }) => {
  const [sLocale, fnSetLocale] = useState<Locale>(fnGetInitialLocale());

  const fnT = useCallback((sKey: string, oReplacements?: Record<string, string | number>): string => {
    let sTranslation = fnGetNestedTranslation(oTranslations[sLocale], sKey) || fnGetNestedTranslation(oTranslations['en'], sKey) || sKey;
    if (oReplacements) {
        Object.keys(oReplacements).forEach(sRKey => {
            sTranslation = sTranslation.replace(`{{${sRKey}}}`, String(oReplacements[sRKey]));
        });
    }
    return sTranslation;
  }, [sLocale]);

  const oValue = useMemo(() => ({
    locale: sLocale,
    setLocale: fnSetLocale,
    t: fnT
  }), [sLocale, fnT]);

  return (
    <I18nContext.Provider value={oValue}>
      {oChildren}
    </I18nContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useI18n = (): I18nContextType => {
  const context = useContext(I18nContext);
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error('useI18n must be used within an I18nProvider');
  }
  return context;
};
